method_description: Reduced-form incumbent high-temperature industrial heat with a recalibrated national-average mix of coal, natural gas and existing biomass/other industrial bioenergy use.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band method roles are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Balance-ready incumbent. Use as a calibration-oriented national average, not a plant-specific archetype.

method_description: High-temperature industrial heat from electric furnaces, plasma/resistive routes or other deep-electrification options.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band method roles are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Useful sensitivity method, but evidence for aggregate national high-temperature electrification remains thin.

method_description: High-temperature heat from hydrogen and/or biomass where direct electrification is not preferred.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band method roles are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Retained because hydrogen is often treated as a candidate for the hardest high-temperature heat niches.
